title: ADR-0072 S4: orchestrator classifies each failure into a failure_policy category + justifies the fix-type per policy (deterministic failureadapter as fallback; Go floor overrides)
summary: The deterministic floor (S1-S3+S6) shipped in d531fc32: verdict-incoherence + escalation halt. S4 adds the JUDGMENT layer the operator asked for — the orchestrator reads an evidence dossier + the failure_policy and emits a structured failure-decision.json {category, level, evidence, justification, action, fix_type}. Authority model (operator-chosen): 'orchestrator decides, Go enforces floor' — the orchestrator's classification is adopted EXCEPT the floor categories (verdict-incoherence, infra-systemic) always halt regardless. Deterministic failureadapter.Decide is the fallback when the agent can't run (retro_always_on_failure).
root_cause: Today the retro->next decision (decideAfterRetro / decideAfterRetroRouted in internal/core/decision_branch.go) is pure Go (failureadapter.Decide), fluent-mode=PROCEED. The orchestrator does not classify failures into policy categories or justify a fix-type. The clean-exit storm showed a broken pipeline can FORGE a task verdict, so the orchestrator must be fed INDEPENDENT evidence (recorded verdict vs on-disk audit+acs vs repetition history), not the recorded verdict alone.
fix: (1) Build a deterministic evidence dossier in internal/core (compose internal/coherence.CheckVerdictCoherence [already shipped] + non-progress counters from state.FailedAt + the bridge error class) -> failure-dossier.json in the cycle workspace, injected into the retro/orchestrator prompt. (2) Extend the retrospective/orchestrator agent instructions (evo:evolve-retrospective / evo:evolve-orchestrator) to emit failure-decision.json {category, level, evidence, justification, action, fix_type} per the failure_policy categories. (3) Consume it in decideAfterRetro* : prefer the orchestrator decision, but the Go floor (SystemFailurePolicy.IsFloor) OVERRIDES to halt, and failureadapter.Decide is the fallback when failure-decision.json is absent/malformed. (4) The chosen fix_type drives the next-cycle recovery target. Config: failure_policy already in policy.json (wired at cmd_cycle). Zero new feature flags.
acceptance: failure-dossier.json written per cycle with recorded-vs-artifact coherence + non-progress counters (contract test).
Orchestrator emits failure-decision.json validated against a schema; a malformed/absent artifact falls back to deterministic failureadapter (contract test both paths).
Go floor override: a failure-decision.json that says 'retry' for a verdict-incoherence cycle is OVERRIDDEN to halt (test).
decideAfterRetro* consumes the decision; the fix_type maps to the recovery target.
go vet ./..., -race on internal/core, apicover -enforce green; no regression in the existing failureadapter/decision_branch tests.
files: skills/ or the retrospective/orchestrator agent instructions (emit failure-decision.json)
notes: Operator emphasis: 'the orchestrator should tell which failures belong to which category and justify what type of fix it should deploy next cycle' + 'give it clear instructions and policy so it decides based on policy'. This is that layer. Feed INDEPENDENT evidence — a forged verdict must not fool the orchestrator (the whole reason the cycle 862->899 storm happened).
